title: Refusal detection end-to-end (stopReason 'refusal' + fail-closed generator)
description: Surface a model refusal as a first-class outcome instead of a silent success. Add an explicit 'refusal' StopReason mapping in the Anthropic and OpenAI-family adapters, detect it at the loop's completion branch, return refused:true on AgenticLoopResult (never throw), and make parseGeneratorResult fail closed: a refusal overrides the filesWritten-implies-success path. Advisory/read-only roles surface the refusal text without failing the run (ADR-5).

successCriteria.description: The Anthropic adapter's normalizeStopReason maps the raw 'refusal' stop reason to StopReason 'refusal' via an explicit case (not the default branch), and the open StopReason union documents 'refusal' as a known value.
The OpenAI-family adapter maps its refusal surface (refusal content / content_filter finish reason) to StopReason 'refusal'.
runAgenticLoop returns { refused: true, stopReason: 'refusal' } with a resolved (not rejected) promise when the client returns a refusal, including when tool results from earlier turns exist.
parseGeneratorResult returns success:false with a refusal note when loopResult.refused === true, even when filesWritten.size > 0 (the refusal guard runs before the filesWritten check).
When no refusal occurs, AgenticLoopResult carries no refused key and all pre-existing loop/generator tests pass unchanged (byte-identical non-refusal path).
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass with the extended types.

nonGoals: Do not add budget, effort, or parallel-tool behavior (later sprints).
Do not throw from the loop on refusal (ADR-5: return, never throw — no catcher exists around runGenerator in pipeline.ts).
Do not change advisory roles' success semantics — they surface refusal text as output.
Do not touch the claude-code adapter's text-only boundary.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: adapter refusal mapping (both families), loop refused flag without throwing, generator fail-closed override of filesWritten, and byte-identical non-refusal behavior.
definitionOfDone: A provider refusal propagates as StopReason 'refusal' through the adapters, sets refused:true and stopReason 'refusal' on AgenticLoopResult without throwing, and a generator sprint that refuses after partial writes is reported success:false. Non-refusal runs are byte-identical. Build, typecheck, lint, and the full suite are green.
assumptions: The Anthropic SDK's raw StopReason already includes 'refusal' (node_modules/@anthropic-ai/sdk .../messages.d.ts) and currently passes through normalizeStopReason's default branch as a raw string; the substantive bug is the loop's completion branch treating any non-tool_use stop as done. Evidence: src/providers/anthropic.ts:32-45, src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts:301.
StopReason (src/providers/types.ts:207) is an open union ('end' | 'tool_use' | 'max_tokens' | 'error' | string) so adding 'refusal' is additive.
No code outside providers/ and agentic-loop.ts reads AgenticLoopResult.stopReason (verified at architecture Checkpoint 4), so the new value cannot break existing consumers.
generatorNotes: Anchors may have drifted — re-locate by pattern. (1) src/providers/anthropic.ts normalizeStopReason (~:32-45): add an explicit case mapping 'refusal' → 'refusal' with a one-line comment that the value is part of the open union. (2) src/providers/openai.ts normalizeStopReason (~:164-175): map the OpenAI refusal surface (message.refusal content and/or finish_reason 'content_filter') → 'refusal'; openai-compat shares this path. (3) src/providers/types.ts: document 'refusal' in the StopReason union comment; add optional refused?: boolean is NOT needed on ChatResponse — the loop derives it. (4) src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts completion branch (~:301, the stopReason !== 'tool_use' return): when response.stopReason === 'refusal', return the normal result shape plus refused: true (spread-conditional so the key is absent otherwise). (5) src/orchestrator/generator-agent.ts parseGeneratorResult (~:181-260): accept the loop result (it already receives it or thread it), and FIRST check refused === true → return success:false with a clear note ('model refused: <finalText excerpt>') before any filesWritten logic. Follow existing test file collocation (*.test.ts next to source) and use fake LLMClient objects returning scripted ChatResponses like existing agentic-loop tests do.
evaluatorNotes: Use a fake client whose chat() returns stopReason 'refusal' (with and without prior tool_use turns) and assert the loop resolves with refused:true, stopReason 'refusal' (sc-1-3). Unit-test normalizeStopReason in both adapters directly (sc-1-1, sc-1-2). For sc-1-4 call parseGeneratorResult with a loop result where refused=true and a non-empty filesWritten set; assert success:false and the note mentions refusal. For sc-1-5 assert a normal completion result has no 'refused' key (use Object.hasOwn) and run the FULL existing suite. Confirm build + typecheck + lint all pass.
